title: Local LLM inference for personal wikis
body_markdown:
## Local LLM inference for personal wikis

Cloud inference works but has three problems for a *personal* wiki:

1. Cost scales with reuse — every query re-reads context.
2. Privacy — personal notes go out to a third party.
3. Latency — ~500ms per call limits batch workflows.

### Hardware target
Mac mini M4 Pro, 64 GB unified RAM, 2 TB. 64 GB is the key spec —
enough for 70B-class models at Q4+ fully in RAM.

### Software target
Ollama or llama.cpp as the backend; whatever lets me swap models
without rewriting the wiki builder. Haiku-class extraction happens
locally; Sonnet-class synthesis still dispatches to Claude for now.
